Package io.fastpix.sdk.models.operations

Class PushMediaSettings

java.lang.Object
io.fastpix.sdk.models.operations.PushMediaSettings
public class PushMediaSettings extends Object
PushMediaSettings

Configuration settings for uploading and processing media on the FastPix platform. These settings define how the uploaded video is handled, including access control, resolution, DRM, and optional metadata. For a complete explanation of how media uploads and processing work, refer to the FastPix Video on Demand Overview.

    • PushMediaSettings

      public PushMediaSettings(@Nullable DirectUploadVideoMediaAccessPolicy accessPolicy, @Nullable Double startTime, @Nullable Double endTime, @Nullable List<Input> inputs, @Nullable Map<String,String> metadata, @Nullable String drmConfigurationId, @Nullable String title, @Nullable String creatorId, @Nullable Subtitles subtitles, @Nullable Boolean optimizeAudio, @Nullable MaxResolution maxResolution, @Nullable MediaQuality mediaQuality, @Nullable Boolean sourceAccess, @Nullable DirectUploadVideoMediaMp4Support mp4Support, @Nullable Summary summary, @Nullable Boolean chapters, @Nullable Boolean namedEntities, @Nullable DirectUploadVideoMediaModeration moderation, @Nullable DirectUploadVideoMediaAccessRestrictions accessRestrictions)

    • PushMediaSettings

      public PushMediaSettings()

  • Method Details

    • accessPolicy

      public Optional<DirectUploadVideoMediaAccessPolicy> accessPolicy()
      Determines if access to the streamed content is kept private, drm or available to all.

    • startTime

      public Optional<Double> startTime()
      Start time indicates where encoding must begin within the video file, in seconds.

    • endTime

      public Optional<Double> endTime()
      End time indicates where encoding must end within the video file, in seconds.

    • inputs

      public Optional<List<Input>> inputs()
      Add one input object at a time. For example, first add a **WatermarkInput** object. If you also need a audio, click **Add item** again and select **AudioInput**.

      Repeat this process for **SubtitleInput** as needed.

    • metadata

      public Optional<Map<String,String>> metadata()
      "Tag a video in "key" : "value" pairs for searchable metadata. Maximum 10 entries, 255 characters each."

    • drmConfigurationId

      public Optional<String> drmConfigurationId()
      UUID of the DRM configuration to be used.

    • title

      public Optional<String> title()
      Title of the media file.

    • creatorId

      public Optional<String> creatorId()
      The unique identifier of the user who created this media.

    • subtitles

      public Optional<Subtitles> subtitles()
      Generates subtitle files for audio/video files.

    • optimizeAudio

      public Optional<Boolean> optimizeAudio()
      Enhance the quality and volume of the audio track. This is available for pre-recorded content only.

    • maxResolution

      public Optional<MaxResolution> maxResolution()
      Determines the highest quality resolution available.

    • mediaQuality

      public Optional<MediaQuality> mediaQuality()
      The quality tier applied to the media.

    • sourceAccess

      public Optional<Boolean> sourceAccess()
      The sourceAccess parameter determines whether the original media file is accessible. Set to true to enable access or false to restrict it

    • mp4Support

      public Optional<DirectUploadVideoMediaMp4Support> mp4Support()
      Generates MP4 video up to 4K ("capped_4k"), m4a audio only ("audioOnly"), or both for offline viewing.

    • summary

      public Optional<Summary> summary()

    • chapters

      public Optional<Boolean> chapters()
      Enable or disable the chapters feature for the media. Set to `true` to enable chapters or `false` to disable.

    • namedEntities

      public Optional<Boolean> namedEntities()
      Enable or disable named entity extraction. Set to `true` to enable or `false` to disable.

    • moderation

      public Optional<DirectUploadVideoMediaModeration> moderation()

    • accessRestrictions

      public Optional<DirectUploadVideoMediaAccessRestrictions> accessRestrictions()

    • builder

      public static PushMediaSettings.Builder builder()

      public PushMediaSettings withAccessPolicy(@Nullable DirectUploadVideoMediaAccessPolicy accessPolicy)
      Determines if access to the streamed content is kept private, drm or available to all.

      Start time indicates where encoding must begin within the video file, in seconds.

      public PushMediaSettings withEndTime(@Nullable Double endTime)
      End time indicates where encoding must end within the video file, in seconds.

    • withInputs

      public PushMediaSettings withInputs(@Nullable List<Input> inputs)
      Add one input object at a time. For example, first add a **WatermarkInput** object. If you also need a audio, click **Add item** again and select **AudioInput**.

      Repeat this process for **SubtitleInput** as needed.

      Enhance the quality and volume of the audio track. This is available for pre-recorded content only.

      Determines the highest quality resolution available.

      The quality tier applied to the media.

      The sourceAccess parameter determines whether the original media file is accessible. Set to true to enable access or false to restrict it

      Generates MP4 video up to 4K ("capped_4k"), m4a audio only ("audioOnly"), or both for offline viewing.

    • withChapters

      public PushMediaSettings withChapters(@Nullable Boolean chapters)
      Enable or disable the chapters feature for the media. Set to `true` to enable chapters or `false` to disable.

      public PushMediaSettings withNamedEntities(@Nullable Boolean namedEntities)
      Enable or disable named entity extraction. Set to `true` to enable or `false` to disable.
